scheme_name: International Centre for Environment Audit and Sustainable Development (iCED)
page_title: International Centre for Environment Audit and Sustainable Development (iCED)

details: The "International Centre for Environment Audit and Sustainable Development (iCED)" was launched by the Indian Audit and Accounts Department, and the Comptroller and Auditor General of India. The iCED Internship Scheme offers a unique opportunity for students specializing in Ocean Science, Environmental Science, Environmental Management, Environment Audit, Environmental Economics, Environmental Law, Public Administration, Public Policy, Sustainable Development, Information Technology, Data Analytics, Architecture, Human Resource Management, Disaster Management, and related fields. This internship will focus on developing awareness about iCED’s work, with a particular emphasis on Blue Economy, Climate Change, and gender-related themes.
The internship duration would be 2 months, which may be extended up to 6 months.
Two interns from one Institution/University would be selected for internship.
benefits: Interns will receive a token remuneration of ₹10,000/- per month to cover conveyance and daily needs.
NOTE: If boarding and lodging are provided by iCED the remuneration will be reduced to ₹5000/- per month.
Certificate will be issued upon satisfactory completion of the internship, assignment submission, and evaluation by the competent authority at iCED.
eligibility: Applicant must be an Indian national.
Applicant must be a Graduate or pursuing Post Graduate/Doctoral courses in one of the following fields
Applicant must be studying at a University/Institution of National or International repute.
Those awarded “Junior Research Fellowship” by the University Grants Commission (UGC) for research in these subjects will be given preference.
If there are more applications than required, the screening of applicants will be done using an appropriate methodology.

online: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Student can email their applications in the prescribed format to: iced@cag.gov.in
Application should be filled in by typing in the above Performa only. No other format will be accepted. Application filled by hand will not be accepted.

Students must share their work and collected materials with iCED in a logically classified manner, including a paper on how iCED/IAAD can use the material. Due to the mentoring provided, intellectual property rights will rest with IA&AD.
A Screening Committee, nominated by the DG, iCED, will scrutinize all applications. Final selection will be made by a Committee of Senior Officers, including the DG, iCED.

documents_required: Letter from the present institution indicating his/her status.
Two Recommendations (not exceeding 500 words each from Professors/Guide/ Employer/Peer).
Statement of Purpose (explaining interest in the internship).

faqs.question: What is the duration of the internship?
faqs.answer: The internship lasts for 2 months, with a possibility of extension up to 6 months.

faqs.question: How many interns are selected from each institution?
faqs.answer: A maximum of two interns are selected from each institution/university.

faqs.question: Is there any remuneration for the interns?
faqs.answer: Yes, interns receive a token remuneration of ₹10,000/- per month. If boarding and lodging are provided, the remuneration is reduced to ₹5000/- per month.

faqs.question: What facilities will be provided to the interns?
faqs.answer: Interns will have access to logistical facilities like space, internet, library, computers, and personal guidance. Concessional accommodation within the campus may also be provided.

faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria for the internship?
faqs.answer: Indian nationals who are graduates or pursuing Postgraduate/Doctoral courses in specified fields and enrolled in reputed universities/institutions.

faqs.question: Is there a preference for certain candidates?
faqs.answer: Yes, candidates awarded “Junior Research Fellowship” by UGC for research in relevant subjects are preferred.

faqs.question: What is the application process for the internship?
faqs.answer: Prepare the required documents, fill out the application form, and submit it to the specified address/email. The applications will be screened and selected by a committee.

faqs.question: What tasks will the interns be expected to perform?
faqs.answer: Interns will assist in ongoing research activities, compile research-related inputs/analyses, assist faculty, develop content, update/modify/analyse databases, and prepare audit guidelines and objectives.

faqs.question: What happens if the internship tenure is terminated early?
faqs.answer: The tenure may be curtailed/ended if the competent authority deems the intern’s association not in the interest of iCED/IA&AD. The decision of the Director General, iCED, will be final.

faqs.question: Can the internship lead to a job offer?
faqs.answer: The internship does not guarantee a job offer or assurance of a job with iCED or any office under the CAG of India.

faqs.question: What should be included in the research proposal?
faqs.answer: The research proposal should contain the research plan, methodology to be followed, and timelines.

faqs.question: How will the work of the interns be monitored?
faqs.answer: Weekly reviews will be conducted by Director (T&R), and the progress will be monitored by the Director General and Director (T&R), iCED.

faqs.question: What are the confidentiality requirements?
faqs.answer: Interns must submit a declaration to respect and maintain confidentiality regarding the issues/information of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India and iCED.

faqs.question: Who will mentor the interns?
faqs.answer: Interns will be mentored and monitored by the Director General and Director (T&R), iCED.

faqs.question: What should the intern’s weekly report contain?
faqs.answer: The weekly report should detail the progress of work, tasks completed, and any challenges faced. It will be reviewed by Director (T&R).
